purpose: The repository serves as a backend WebAPI for loan management in a lending platform, specifically handling the selling and purchasing of loans. It enables MPLs (Marketplace Lenders) to purchase loans and manage loan lifecycle operations including transfers, fees, interest accruals, and investor relationship management.

apis.purpose: Get details for a specific loan
Generate pre-sale or post-sale loan reports

dataEntities.name: Loan
dataEntities.description: Represents a loan with its details, status, and relationship to other entities

dataEntities.name: LoanAction
dataEntities.description: Tracks actions performed on loans such as purchases, interest accruals, and fee collections

dataEntities.name: Transfer
dataEntities.description: Represents a financial transfer between accounts with status tracking

dataEntities.name: Contract
dataEntities.description: Defines relationship between MPL and bank with terms for loan operations

dataEntities.name: Account
dataEntities.description: Banking account used for transfers and financial operations

dataEntities.name: Batch
dataEntities.description: Groups multiple loans for bulk processing

dataEntities.name: InterestHistory
dataEntities.description: Tracks interest accruals for loans over time

dataEntities.name: VolumeFeeMonthlyMinimum
dataEntities.description: Tracks minimum fee requirements for MPLs

messagingPatterns.name: TransferOutbox
messagingPatterns.description: Ensures reliable processing of transfer requests to external systems

messagingPatterns.name: NotificationOutbox
messagingPatterns.description: Manages notifications to be sent to external systems

messagingPatterns.name: SQS Message Handling
messagingPatterns.description: Processes messages from AWS SQS for reliable background processing

findings.title: Insecure credentials handling in docker-compose
findings.description: Hard-coded database credentials are present in docker-compose.yml. Credentials should be moved to environment variables or secrets management.

findings.title: Missing idempotency in outbox processors
findings.description: While some outbox processors check for duplicates, others don't implement proper idempotency checks, which could lead to duplicate operations if processors are restarted.

ambiguities: Exact purpose and structure of the MessageDispatcherService and how it relates to the outbox processors
How loan approval workflow is structured and what triggers state transitions
Specifics around the auto-purchase configuration and eligibility criteria
Exact contract model with MPLs and how terms are enforced
How reconciliation with external systems is managed and resolved when discrepancies are found
